guardrail_evaluations.explanation: The proposed 30% discount exceeds the mid-market soft ceiling of 20%. This is a yellow flag — the AE should be prepared to justify why a retention concession of this magnitude is warranted versus alternatives.

The proposed discount is exactly at the 30% Finance approval threshold (operator is <=, so 30 = 30 passes). However, Finance sign-off is still required before this deal can be countersigned — do not proceed without that approval in hand.
No ramp schedule is present in this deal (ramp_length_months = 0), so the ramp sign-off guardrail does not apply. This is clean.

Estimated gross margin of ~14.3% is well below the 40% warn floor. Under the 40%-at-list working assumption, COGS is $240K and the proposed price of $280K leaves only ~$40K of gross profit — a very thin cushion.

BLOCKER: Estimated margin of 14.3% falls below the 25% absolute floor. Deal cannot proceed as structured — concessions must be reshaped before re-quoting. The proposed price of $280K against an estimated COGS of $240K yields only ~$40K gross profit.

alternative_structures.label: Right-Size Commit + 24-Month Lock-In
alternative_structures.description: Reset the annual commit to $320K (20% discount from list) in exchange for a 24-month term commitment. This acknowledges under-utilization without a full 30% haircut, and the extended term provides ACV durability. Retain the overage/usage-based upside above the new commit at $0.0008/unit. Keep net-60 payment terms as a concession to close.
alternative_structures.rationale: Lands exactly at the absolute margin floor of 25%, satisfying the block_absolute guardrail. Swaps a pure price cut for a structural retention commitment — the customer gets cost relief and predictability; Clay gets two years of contracted revenue from a flagship logo. The 20% discount still requires Finance sign-off but avoids the block_absolute trigger.

alternative_structures.label: Usage-Only Reset with Consumption Floor
alternative_structures.description: Convert the entire contract to a usage-based model with a $300K annual spend floor (25% discount) and an overage rate of $0.0008/unit. Include a built-in Year-2 escalator of 8% if consumption exceeds 80% of the floor in Year 1. This aligns pricing directly to Northbeam's realized value and eliminates the 'paying for unused credits' objection.
alternative_structures.rationale: Directly addresses the under-utilization complaint with a structure the customer can rationally defend internally ('we only pay for what we use'). The Year-2 escalator creates upside if Northbeam's mortgage volumes recover as rates ease. At $300K estimated margin is ~20% — still below the warn threshold but above the deepest churn scenario. Requires Finance approval on both the discount and the hybrid model conversion.

alternative_structures.label: Discount-for-Value Exchange: Co-Marketing + Reduced Concession
alternative_structures.description: Hold the base renewal at $340K (15% discount) and offer a co-marketing credit of $20K in professional services (onboarding, feature adoption workshops targeting the 3 unadopted features) in lieu of additional cash discount. Pair with a public case study commitment and a named reference slot for the fintech vertical. Payment terms stay net-60.
alternative_structures.rationale: Protects margin above the 25% absolute floor while addressing root-cause churn risk (low feature adoption). The PS investment is a separate revenue line at potentially higher margin and directly drives product stickiness — reducing churn risk more effectively than a pure price cut. Co-marketing ROI from a flagship fintech logo offsets the PS cost. This is the strongest margin and retention story but requires the AE to reframe the negotiation from 'price' to 'value realization.'

pricing.reasoning_summary: The proposed 30% discount to $280K triggers the block_absolute gross margin guardrail — estimated margin is ~14.3%, well below the 25% floor — meaning this deal cannot proceed as structured. The discount also blows through the 20% mid-market soft ceiling (warn), though it sits exactly at the 30% Finance approval threshold and technically passes that specific operator check. All margin estimates are derived from a working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin (i.e., COGS = $240K); the CFO should validate actual COGS before treating the 14.3% figure as definitive. Given the customer's weak health score (42/100), a real competitive threat, and macro headwinds in mortgage origination, a pure price cut is the least defensible structure — the alternative structures above prioritize margin recovery, term commitment, and adoption-led retention.

performance_obligations.name: Platform subscription (committed base)
performance_obligations.description: Annual subscription to the Northbeam platform at a reduced $280K commit for the 12-month renewal term.
performance_obligations.rationale: Customer receives and consumes the benefit of the SaaS platform over the contract term; distinct under ASC 606-10-25-19.

performance_obligations.name: Usage overage above commit
performance_obligations.description: Consumption-based charges at $0.0008/unit for usage exceeding the new $280K commit.
performance_obligations.rationale: Overage represents a separate stand-ready/usage right recognized as usage occurs under the series-of-distinct-services guidance and the variable consideration allocation exception (ASC 606-10-32-40).

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: Overage at $0.0008/unit is variable. Given customer used only 60% of prior commit, overage is unlikely; recognize as incurred under the usage allocation exception rather than estimating upfront.
The 30% commit reduction is fixed in the renewal contract ($280K) and is not itself variable, but signals continued under-utilization risk for future renewals.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: This renewal is itself a new contract; no MFN, TFC, or expansion-pricing-lock clauses are present that would re-open the contract mid-term. Net-60 payment terms and overage shift do not create modification risk under ASC 606-10-25-10.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Ratable recognition of $280K committed subscription over 12-month term ($23,333/month).
Overage recognized only if/when usage exceeds commit; expected to be $0 given 60% historical utilization.

red_flags.label: Significant financing component risk (net-60)
red_flags.explanation: Extension to net-60 is generally below the one-year practical expedient threshold (ASC 606-10-32-18), so no financing component adjustment is required, but document the assessment.

red_flags.label: Material right / future concession precedent
red_flags.explanation: Granting a 30% renewal discount tied to under-utilization may create an implicit expectation of future concessions, potentially constituting a material right in subsequent renewals (ASC 606-10-55-42).

red_flags.label: Customer credit risk
red_flags.explanation: Health score 42, recent layoffs, secondary down-round, and sector headwinds raise collectibility considerations under ASC 606-10-25-1(e); confirm collectibility threshold is met before contract inception.

red_flags.label: Breakage / unused commit
red_flags.explanation: Historical 60% utilization suggests the new commit may again go partially unused; since commit is non-refundable and recognized ratably, no breakage estimate is needed, but disclose pattern.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a straightforward 12-month renewal with a fixed $280K commit recognized ratably and a usage-based overage recognized as incurred under the allocation exception. Primary accounting considerations are the net-60 financing-component assessment and the precedent risk of a discount-driven concession that could be viewed as a material right in future renewals. Collectibility should be re-confirmed given customer health signals. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests a 30% reduction to the committed baseline from $400K to $280K ACV for the FY26 renewal term, citing only 60% consumption of prior-year commit.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Accepting a 30% commit reduction sets a permanent new floor: next renewal negotiation anchors to $280K, not $400K, making full recovery to prior ACV structurally unlikely. Combined with 40% under-utilization, the reduction rewards non-adoption rather than driving engagement — and the CSM has not tied the concession to any usage improvement commitment or success plan milestone, so churn risk persists at the lower price.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay agrees to reduce the committed ACV to $310,000 (22% reduction vs. list) conditioned on: (a) Customer completing an agreed joint Success Plan within 30 days of execution targeting activation of no fewer than three (3) currently unadopted paid features by Month 6; (b) a mid-term checkpoint at Month 6 in which, if utilization reaches 80% of new commit, Customer is entitled to request a one-time overage credit of up to 5% of ACV; and (c) the renewal baseline for FY27 reverting to no less than $340,000 absent mutual written agreement. The 30% reduction to $280K is not acceptable as a unilateral concession without binding utilization commitments.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept $280K commit only if Customer executes a binding Success Plan with named internal champions, a 6-month utilization checkpoint at 75% of new commit, and a contractual FY27 floor of $310K (i.e., no further reduction without cause). Do not accept $280K flat with no strings attached.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered commit reductions on renewals before in documented at-risk situations, but typically capped at 20% and tied to a co-signed success plan; a 30% unconditioned reduction is outside prior precedent.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests that any consumption above the new $280K commit be billed at a pure usage-based rate ($0.0008/unit) rather than the prior hybrid model, with no minimum overage floor.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Switching to pure usage-based overage removes Clay's ability to capture predictable upside if Northbeam's origination volume recovers — the customer gets optionality on growth while Clay bears the downside of commit reduction. At $0.0008/unit with no floor, a customer in a macro-recovery environment could generate meaningful overage without any commitment, effectively giving them a free call option on expanded usage.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Overage above the committed baseline shall be billed at $0.0008 per unit; however, (a) overage is calculated on a quarterly basis with a minimum quarterly overage charge of $5,000 once overage threshold is crossed, and (b) if cumulative overage in any rolling 90-day period exceeds 25% of the quarterly committed baseline, Customer and Clay shall negotiate in good faith a revised commit tier within 30 days. Usage-based overage pricing shall not be used as the basis for any future renewal pricing benchmark.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept usage-based overage at $0.0008/unit with no quarterly floor, but include explicit contract language stating that overage pricing is not precedent-setting for future committed tiers and may not be cited as a comparable in future renewal negotiations.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Hybrid-to-usage-based overage switches have been accepted in at-risk renewals; the precedent risk is the absence of a floor, which Clay has previously required.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests extension of payment terms from net-30 to net-60, citing cash flow constraints consistent with their cost-discipline posture.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Net-60 on a $280K ACV deal represents ~$23K in float per invoice cycle — manageable in isolation, but the customer's signals (down-round secondary, 8% RIF, declining origination volume) elevate counterparty credit risk. If Northbeam's runway deteriorates, net-60 increases Clay's exposure window for collection on each invoice by 30 days. Combined with the commit reduction, this is a cash-flow double-compression.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay agrees to net-45 payment terms (not net-60) on invoices issued after execution, provided Customer pays the first invoice (prorated to cover Month 1–3) within net-30 of the execution date as a good-faith payment. Annual invoicing (full year upfront) at a net-30 term shall be offered as an alternative in exchange for an additional 2% discount, which may be applied against the agreed ACV.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept net-60 only if the full annual invoice is issued upfront (i.e., one invoice for $280K due within 60 days of execution), eliminating quarterly billing risk. Do not accept net-60 on a quarterly billing cadence.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Net-45 has been accepted as a compromise for at-risk mid-market renewals; net-60 on quarterly billing for a customer showing credit stress signals is outside standard Clay policy.

standard_clauses_affirmed: Warranty: no non-standard warranty language surfaced; assumed standard 90-day SLA applies
Indemnification: no customer-proposed changes noted; mutual standard assumed
Data residency: no non-standard data residency clause surfaced; US-standard assumed
Term length: 12-month renewal term is standard
redline.one_line_summary: We can retain Northbeam, but the 30% commit cut must be conditioned on a binding success plan and FY27 floor — and net-60 should be countered with net-45 or upfront annual billing given their deteriorating credit signals.
redline.reasoning_summary: All three customer-requested clauses carry compounding risk: the commit reduction sets a dangerous precedent floor with no utilization accountability, the usage-based overage switch hands Northbeam a free growth option, and net-60 extends collection exposure precisely when the customer's financial health signals (down-round secondary, 8% RIF, declining origination volume, CRO cost-discipline posture) are at their weakest. The YC competitor threat is real but narrow — a W26 startup unlikely has Northbeam's enterprise compliance, audit trail, and fintech-grade SLA history, which is Clay's primary negotiating leverage. Recommended path is a conditioned $310K counter with success plan milestones, net-45 or upfront billing, and a contractual FY27 floor to prevent further anchor erosion.

required_approvers.rationale: Default approval required for all deals; AE Manager (Marcus Reilly) is the first step in the approval chain.

Proposed discount of 30% exceeds the 25% threshold, triggering mandatory CFO review; Pricing Agent confirms this sits exactly at the Finance approval threshold (30% = 30% operator check) but notes block_absolute margin guardrail (14.3% margin vs. 25% floor) means the deal structure itself cannot proceed without redesign.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Non-standard payment terms route to Finance
required_approvers.rationale: Customer requested extension from net-30 to net-60, which is explicitly flagged in the non_standard_clauses and requires Finance review for cash-flow and collectibility impact given Northbeam's weak health score (42/100).
Deal ACV of $280K falls within the $100K–$500K band, requiring RevOps eye on structure consistency and renewal baseline anchoring risk.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Redline priority 'high' and non-standard clauses flagged
required_approvers.rationale: Redline Agent flagged three high/medium-risk clauses (commit_reduction_30pct, switch_to_usage_based_for_overage, payment_terms_net_60) with overall_redline_priority='high'; commit_reduction and payment_terms are explicitly present in non_standard_clauses, requiring Legal review of precedent risk and contract modification language.

blockers_to_address_first: CRITICAL: Pricing Agent block_absolute guardrail — proposed 30% discount yields ~14.3% gross margin, well below 25% absolute floor. Deal structure cannot proceed as quoted. AE must select one of Pricing Agent's alternative structures (Right-Size Commit to $320K at 20% discount + 24-month lock, Usage-Only Reset at $300K with consumption floor, or Value-Exchange at $340K + $20K PS investment) before submitting for approval.

CRITICAL: Redline Agent flagged commit_reduction_30pct as 'high risk' without binding success-plan milestones or FY27 floor. Legal must not approve $280K baseline as a unilateral concession — Redline Agent's suggested counter is $310K conditioned on Success Plan, 6-month checkpoint, and FY27 floor of $340K. Fallback is $280K only with Success Plan + 75% utilization checkpoint + FY27 floor of $310K.

CRITICAL: Payment terms net-60 on declining-health customer ($280K reduced ACV, 8% RIF, down-round secondary, declining mortgage volumes). Redline Agent recommends net-45 or upfront annual billing instead. Finance must confirm collectibility assessment and approve net-60 only if deal structure moves to upfront annual invoicing.

CRITICAL: Northbeam health score is 42/100 with material downside signals (declining origination volume, workforce reduction, secondary down-round). ASC 606 Agent flagged collectibility consideration (info severity); CFO must confirm that collectibility threshold is met before contract inception, particularly given the extended net-60 terms and reduced ACV.

HIGH: Redline Agent precedent warning — 30% unilateral commit reduction without utilization accountability rewards non-adoption and sets a dangerous anchor for FY27 renewal. Legal must enforce success-plan conditioning and explicit FY27 floor language to prevent further erosion.

MEDIUM: Usage-based overage switch ($0.0008/unit with no minimum floor) removes predictable upside and hands customer a free call option on growth. Redline Agent recommends minimum quarterly overage floor ($5K) and explicit language that overage is not precedent-setting for future committed tiers. Legal must negotiate this carve-out.
approval.one_line_summary: This deal cannot proceed as structured due to a block_absolute margin guardrail (14.3% vs. 25% floor); AE must restructure via Pricing Agent's alternatives before routing to approvals. If restructured, the chain is AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + CFO + Legal (parallel) over ~5 business days, conditional on Legal enforcement of success-plan milestones, FY27 floor, net-45 or upfront billing, and collectibility confirmation given Northbeam's deteriorating health (42/100 score, 8% RIF, down-round secondary, macro headwinds).
approval.reasoning_summary: The deal is mechanically well-mapped to the approval matrix (five rules fire cleanly: discount >25%, ACV in $100K–$500K, non-standard payment terms, redline priority high, and default AE Manager step). However, the Pricing Agent's block_absolute guardrail is a hard stop — the proposed 30% discount to $280K yields only ~14.3% margin vs. the 25% floor, making this structure non-viable. The AE must choose one of three alternative structures before submission. If restructured (e.g., $310K–$320K with success-plan conditioning), the approval chain is standard (AE Manager → parallel Finance/CFO/RevOps/Legal over 5 days). Legal's role is elevated due to redline priority 'high' and three flagged clauses requiring conditioning: commit reduction must be tied to success plan and FY27 floor; usage-based overage needs a quarterly minimum and non-precedent language; net-60 should be countered to net-45 or upfront billing given Northbeam's material credit risk signals (health score 42, 8% RIF, down-round secondary, declining origination volumes in macro downturn).

text.text: Deal Review: FY26 Renewal — Commit Reduction Request · Northbeam Mortgage

fields.text:
*Approval Chain*
AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + CFO + Legal (parallel) · ~5 biz days

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know*

• *HARD STOP — deal cannot proceed as structured.* The proposed $280K / 30% discount yields ~14.3% gross margin, triggering the block_absolute guardrail (floor: 25%). AE must reprice before submission. Pricing Agent's recommended paths: Right-Size Commit to $320K + 24-month lock (25% margin, clears floor); Usage-Only Reset at $300K with consumption floor (~20% margin); or Value-Exchange at $340K + $20K PS investment (~29% margin). Best-margin path is the Value-Exchange.

• *Commit reduction must be conditioned — not a unilateral concession.* Accepting $280K flat with no strings rewards non-adoption (customer used only 60% of prior commit, 3 of 5 features unadopted) and anchors FY27 to $280K. Redline Agent's counter: $310K conditioned on a binding Success Plan, 6-month utilization checkpoint at 75–80% of new commit, and a contractual FY27 floor of $310–340K. Legal must enforce this before countersigning.

• *Net-60 payment terms carry elevated credit risk and require upfront billing or a counter to net-45.* Northbeam signals are deteriorating — 8% RIF (Mar 2026), down-round Series C secondary, declining origination volume for three straight quarters, and a health score of 42/100. Finance must confirm collectibility under ASC 606-10-25-1(e) and should require either upfront annual invoicing at net-60 or quarterly billing at net-45. Do not accept net-60 on quarterly billing.

elements.text: AE: Jamal Okafor · Manager: Marcus Reilly | review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback: Deal Review: FY26 Renewal — Northbeam Mortgage | ACV: $280K (30% off $400K list) | Margin: ~14.3% — BELOW 25% absolute floor; deal cannot proceed as structured. AE must reprice (recommended path: $320K + 24-month lock or $340K + PS value exchange) before routing approvals. Commit reduction must be conditioned on a binding Success Plan and FY27 floor — no unilateral concession. Net-60 payment terms require Finance collectibility sign-off given Northbeam's deteriorating health (42/100, 8% RIF, down-round secondary). Approval chain: AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + CFO + Legal (parallel), ~5 business days. AE: Jamal Okafor.

ae_email_draft.subject: [Northbeam Mortgage] Pricing review complete — deal BLOCKED, 3 actions required before submission
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Jamal, the deal review is done and the $280K / 30% discount structure is a **hard block** — estimated gross margin comes in at ~14.3%, well below the 25% absolute floor, meaning this cannot go to approval as quoted.

Here are the three things you need to do before this deal can move:

1. **Reprice using one of the three Pricing Agent alternatives.** Best path is the Right-Size Commit at $320K (20% discount) with a 24-month lock-in — this lands exactly at the margin floor and gives you a retention story beyond a pure price cut. If the customer won't budge past $310K, that's the fallback *only* with a binding Success Plan, a 6-month utilization checkpoint (75% of new commit), and a contractual FY27 floor of $340K written in.
2. **Counter net-60 with net-45 or upfront annual billing.** Northbeam had a down-round secondary, cut 8% of staff, and their CRO is on record hammering cost discipline — Finance will not approve net-60 on quarterly billing for this profile. Upfront annual invoice at net-30 is the cleanest path; net-45 is the compromise.
3. **Get Legal's success-plan language drafted before you go back to the customer.** The commit reduction must be conditioned on named champions, feature adoption milestones (3 of 5 unadopted features active by Month 6), and the FY27 floor — do not let the CSM communicate an unconditional concession to Northbeam while this is in flight.

Once repriced and redlined, the approval chain is: **Marcus Reilly (AE Manager) → RevOps + Finance + CFO + Legal in parallel** — CFO and Legal both hold veto, and the expected cycle is ~5 business days. Your close date is May 18, so you have very little runway to waste on a structure that will get kicked back at step one.

customer_email_draft.subject: Northbeam FY26 Renewal — Clay's Position on Your Three Asks
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We understand the pressure you're facing: mortgage origination headwinds, a tighter cost environment, and a need to demonstrate spend discipline internally. We want to find a path that works for both sides and keeps this relationship intact for the long term.

On the commit reduction: we can't accept a flat 30% cut to $280K without any conditions — that structure doesn't hold up on our end financially, and frankly it doesn't address the root issue, which is that three of your five paid features are going unused. Our counter is a revised commit of $310K (a 22% reduction from list), conditioned on a joint Success Plan executed within 30 days of signing that targets activation of those three unadopted features by Month 6, and a contractual FY27 renewal floor of $340K to protect both sides from further anchor erosion.

On overage pricing: we're aligned on moving to usage-based billing above the commit at $0.0008/unit, with one modification — we need a minimum quarterly overage charge of $5K once the overage threshold is crossed, and explicit contract language confirming overage rates are not precedent-setting for future committed tiers.

On payment terms: we can meet you at net-45 rather than net-60; alternatively, if you invoice annually upfront, we'll hold net-30 and apply an additional 2% discount against the agreed ACV — that structure may also be easier for your team to defend internally as a cost-reduction win.

The YC competitor you're evaluating is early-stage and will have real gaps in fintech compliance, audit trail depth, and enterprise SLA coverage — we'd welcome a direct comparison conversation if that's useful before your CRO signs anything.

Your CRO's timeline puts us at roughly May 18th; our internal approvals run about five business days once we have a signed term sheet, so we'd need alignment on these terms by May 12th to hit that window. Can we get 30 minutes this week to align on the Success Plan structure and close the gap on commit level?

approval_review_one_pager.title: FY26 Renewal — Northbeam Mortgage Commit Reduction Request

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Northbeam Mortgage (flagship fintech logo, $400K prior ACV) is requesting a 30% commit reduction to $280K, a shift to usage-based overage, and net-60 payment terms on renewal. The deal as submitted **cannot proceed** — it triggers a block_absolute margin guardrail (14.3% estimated margin vs. 25% floor) and must be restructured before routing to approvals.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| List price | $400,000 |
| Proposed price (as submitted) | $280,000 |
| Effective discount | 30% |
| Estimated gross margin | ~14.3% |
| Margin floor (absolute) | 25% — **BLOCKED** |
| Mid-market soft ceiling | 20% — **EXCEEDED** |

**Alternative structures to unblock (select one before re-submitting):**
- **Option A — Right-Size + 24-mo Lock:** $320K (20% discount), 24-month term, margin ~25%. Hits the floor exactly; preferred.
- **Option B — Usage-Only Reset:** $300K floor (25% discount) + Year-2 escalator at 8% if >80% consumed, margin ~20%.
- **Option C — Value Exchange:** $340K (15% discount) + $20K PS co-marketing credit targeting 3 unadopted features, margin ~29.4%. Strongest margin story.

All three alternatives still require Finance sign-off on discount.

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**Redline — HIGH overall priority:**
- **Commit reduction (High risk):** 30% unilateral cut with no utilization accountability sets a dangerous FY27 anchor at $280K. Counter: $310K conditioned on binding Success Plan, 6-month utilization checkpoint (75–80%), and contractual FY27 floor of $340K. Fallback: $280K only with Success Plan + FY27 floor of $310K.
- **Usage-based overage (Medium risk):** No minimum floor hands Northbeam a free growth call option. Counter: $5K quarterly minimum overage floor + explicit non-precedent language for future renewal tiers.
- **Net-60 payment terms (Medium risk):** $23K/invoice float on a customer showing material credit stress (8% RIF, down-round secondary, declining origination volumes). Counter: net-45, or accept net-60 only on upfront annual invoicing.

**ASC 606 flags:**
- **Net-60 financing component (Warn):** Below 1-year practical expedient threshold — no adjustment required, but assessment must be documented.
- **Material right / concession precedent (Warn):** 30% utilization-linked discount may create implicit expectation of future concessions, potentially constituting a material right in subsequent renewals (ASC 606-10-55-42).
- **Collectibility (Info):** Health score 42/100, recent layoffs, down-round secondary — CFO must confirm collectibility threshold is met before contract inception.
- **Breakage (Info):** 60% historical utilization suggests partial commit underuse; no breakage estimate needed on non-refundable commit, but pattern should be disclosed.

**Customer health signals (all negative):**
- 8% workforce reduction (Mar 2026), focused on back-office and loan officers
- Mortgage origination volume down 14% YoY for third consecutive quarter
- Series C secondary closed below 2023 mark — effectively a down-round
- CRO cited "cost discipline" 8x on Q4 investor call
- Health score: 42/100; 3 of 5 paid features unadopted
- YC W26 competitor pitching at ~60% below current pricing

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
**Pre-condition (must complete before routing):** AE (Jamal Okafor) must select and re-quote using one of the three alternative structures above to clear the block_absolute margin guardrail.

| Step | Approver | Role | Veto? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Marcus Reilly | AE Manager | No |
| 2 (parallel) | RevOps | ACV $100K–$500K band | No |
| 2 (parallel) | Finance | Non-standard payment terms + discount | No |
| 2 (parallel) | CFO | Discount >25%, block_absolute margin, collectibility | **Yes** |
| 2 (parallel) | Legal | Redline priority High, 3 flagged non-standard clauses | **Yes** |

**Estimated cycle time:** ~5 business days after AE re-submits restructured deal.
**Close date:** May 18, 2026 — 9 business days from today. Tight but executable if restructure is submitted by May 6.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**HOLD — restructure required before approval routing.**

The deal as submitted is a hard block: 14.3% margin vs. 25% absolute floor. The recommended path is **Option A (Right-Size + 24-month Lock at $320K)**, conditioned on a binding Success Plan with feature-adoption milestones and a contractual FY27 floor — this retains a strategically important fintech logo, satisfies the margin floor, and converts a pure price cut into a structural retention commitment that reduces future anchor erosion risk.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to warm tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 3 flagged redlines (3 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~5 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: The proposed 30% discount to $280K breaches the block_absolute margin guardrail at ~14.3% vs. the 25% floor, making the deal non-viable as structured and forcing a counter in the $310K–$320K range. ASC 606 treatment is otherwise clean — ratable commit plus usage overage under the allocation exception — but net-60 raises a financing-component question and the discount creates material-right precedent risk for future renewals. All three redlined clauses (commit reduction, usage overage switch, net-60) compound risk against a customer with a 42 health score, 8% RIF, and a down-round secondary, so each must be conditioned with success-plan milestones, a quarterly minimum with non-precedent language, and net-45 or upfront billing. The approval chain maps cleanly through AE Manager → RevOps → Finance → CFO → Legal over five days, but cannot start until Pricing's hard stop is cleared. Recommendation: hold and re-scope to a conditioned $310K counter before routing.

Coastline's regulatory audit window pushed procurement out 9 months. Champion left mid-cycle. Logged for re-engagement Q4 2026.

similar_deals.deal_name: Q2 Expansion — Inbound SDR AI Enrichment
similar_deals.decision_note: Ramp's RevOps wants to add Clay enrichment + AI scoring across their inbound SDR pipeline (45 reps). They're asking for an MFN limited to three named competitors (Apollo, Clearbit, ZoomInfo Sales OS), a 99.95% uptime SLA with service cre…

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage trims 8% of staff in operations and loan-officer roles
signals.summary: Northbeam Mortgage confirmed an 8% workforce reduction primarily affecting back-office operations and loan-officer roles, citing the prolonged rate environment and a need to extend runway through 2027.

signals.headline: YC-backed mortgage-origination startup launches at 60% below incumbents
signals.summary: A Y Combinator W26 fintech launched a mortgage-origination platform priced at roughly 60% below incumbents like Northbeam and Better, targeting mid-market regional lenders with an AI-first underwriting workflow.

signals.headline: Mortgage origination volume falls for third straight quarter as rates hold high
signals.summary: Industry data shows mortgage origination volume down 14% YoY in Q1 2026 as the Fed holds rates above 5%. Mid-market lenders cite a third straight quarter of demand softness.

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage CRO emphasizes 'cost discipline' on Q4 investor call
signals.summary: On the Q4 2025 investor call, Northbeam's CRO highlighted 'unit economics' and 'cost discipline' eight times, signaling tighter scrutiny on every renewal and procurement contract through fiscal 2026.

signals.headline: Northbeam Mortgage extends Series C runway with $40M secondary
signals.summary: Northbeam closed a $40M structured secondary led by an existing investor, framed as runway extension rather than a primary raise. The transaction valued the company below its 2023 Series C mark.
